prompt: The Externalizable class fails to deserialize. What is the bug?
choices: Externalizable requires a public no-arg constructor; deserialization instantiates the class using it before calling readExternal
writeExternal must be called before readExternal
int value must be declared transient for Externalizable
explanation: Unlike Serializable (which bypasses the constructor), Externalizable requires a public no-arg constructor. During deserialization, the JVM calls new ExternBug() (no-arg) to create the instance, then calls readExternal(). Without a no-arg constructor, an InvalidClassException is thrown. Fix: add public ExternBug() {}
